project.name: Allegro PoC — Swing + WebSocket + Vue.js
project.description: A multi-tier modernization proof-of-concept combining a Java Swing desktop application (MVP pattern), a Node.js WebSocket broadcast server, and a Vue.js web client.

body.note: Unnamed variable — side-effect wiring only; PocPresenter constructor wires all listeners
Keeps JVM alive indefinitely — the Swing EDT owns the UI thread

type_declarations.name: EventListener
type_declarations.design_pattern: Observer (functional interface — can be a lambda)

type_declarations.name: ModelProperties
type_declarations.note: 13 constants — each maps to a view field, a model ValueModel entry, and an OpenAPI PostObject property. FEMALE/MALE/DIVERSE use ValueModel<Boolean>; all others use ValueModel<String>.

type_declarations.name: PocModel
type_declarations.design_pattern: MVP — Model layer; owns all domain state

body.description: Populates EnumMap with null-initialized ValueModel entries for all 13 ModelProperties constants
Log all model field values to stdout
Build serialization-safe String map from typed model entries

type_declarations.name: ViewData
type_declarations.note: Empty placeholder — reserved for future view-specific data transfer object

type_declarations.name: PocPresenter
type_declarations.design_pattern: MVP — Presenter layer; mediates View ↔ Model

arguments.design_pattern: Observer callback — resets view after successful HTTP response
Observer — listens to Swing document changes

try_block.name: model
try_block.note: Re-fetches model — shadows outer model variable

methods.name: changedUpdate
methods.note: No-op — styled document changes not relevant for plain text fields

type_declarations.name: PocView
type_declarations.design_pattern: MVP — View layer; pure UI construction, zero business logic

methods.name: initUI
methods.summary: Builds GridBagLayout panel (6 cols × 6 rows). Row 0: firstName, name, dateOfBirth. Row 1: gender radio group (span=5). Row 2: street, zip, ort. Row 3: iban, bic, validFrom. Row 4: textArea (span=6, 200×400px). Row 5: button. Frame: 800×650, EXIT_ON_CLOSE, visible.

arguments.note: Empty HTTP handler — acts as WebSocket upgrade target only

body.note: Broadcasts to ALL connected clients simultaneously
Removes this client from the broadcast list using closure-captured index

files.design_pattern: Vue SFC with real-time WebSocket integration and in-memory search

sections.description: Form with 19 v-model bindings for person data entry
Table of payment recipients for selected person
Button: sends selected result over WebSocket

return_fields.name: search_space
return_fields.description: Hard-coded mock dataset — 5 persons, each with zahlungsempfaenger sub-array

condition.description: Matches on last name, first name, zip (exact), ort, street, or house number (case-insensitive contains)

body.name: obj_to_send
body.note: Deep clone via JSON round-trip: JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(e))
Every textarea keystroke sends a WebSocket message

info.title: Allegro PoC
info.description: API documentation for the Allegro modernization PoC.

paths.summary: A simple Post resource returning what was sent.

schemas.name: PostObject
schemas.note: All 13 properties exactly mirror the ModelProperties enum constant names

participants.model: PocModel — holds all domain state in EnumMap<ModelProperties, ValueModel<?>>; performs HTTP POST via HttpBinService; fires events via EventEmitter
participants.view: PocView — pure Swing UI; exposes protected field references so presenter can attach listeners; no logic whatsoever
participants.presenter: PocPresenter — attaches DocumentListeners and ChangeListeners to view fields; updates model on change; subscribes to EventEmitter to reset view after model action completes

participants.subject: EventEmitter — maintains List<EventListener>; loops on emit()
participants.observer_interface: EventListener — single-method interface: void onEvent(String eventData); compatible with lambda
participants.concrete_observer: Lambda in PocPresenter constructor — clears form fields and populates textArea with HTTP response body

participants.usage: 10 × ValueModel<String> for text fields; 3 × ValueModel<Boolean> for radio buttons, all stored in EnumMap keyed by ModelProperties

participants.broker: WebsocketServer.js — maintains clients[] array; on every message broadcasts to all clients
participants.publishers: Search.vue sendMessage() — sends person data on button click; also on every textarea keystroke via watcher
participants.subscribers: All currently connected WebSocket clients receive the broadcast (including potential future Java Swing integration)

design_patterns.java_impl: PocPresenter.bind() attaches DocumentListener (for text) / ChangeListener (for radio) to each UI component; syncs to ValueModel on every edit
design_patterns.vue_impl: v-model directives on all form inputs reactively sync with formdata object

design_patterns.participant: HttpBinService — encapsulates raw HttpURLConnection setup, javax.json streaming serialization, response reading; consumed solely by PocModel.action()
ActionListener lambda on PocView.button — when clicked, delegates to PocModel.action(), wrapping checked exceptions in RuntimeException

cross_tier.payload: JSON matching PostObject schema (all ModelProperties as string properties)

cross_tier.note: Triggers UI reset and textArea population

user_input_to_model: JTextField / JTextArea keystroke → DocumentListener.insertUpdate or removeUpdate → e.getDocument().getText(0, length) → ValueModel<String>.setField(content)
model_to_api: PocModel.action(): iterates ModelProperties.values() → builds HashMap<String,String> (toString() on every field)
HttpBinService: opens HttpURLConnection POST to http://localhost:8080/post, serializes map to JSON via javax.json streaming API
Reads response with Scanner(inputStream).useDelimiter('\\A').next() → returns responseBody String
api_response_to_view: Presenter lambda: view.textArea.setText(responseBody), clears firstName/name/dateOfBirth/zip/ort/street/iban/bic/validFrom, resets female=true, male=false, diverse=false
vue_search_to_websocket: User types in form fields → v-model reactive binding → formdata object updated
User clicks 'Suchen' → searchPerson() → iterates search_space → pushes matching elements to search_result
User clicks table row → selectResult(item) → selected_result = item
User clicks IBAN/BIC row → zahlungsempfaengerSelected(item) → zahlungsempfaenger_selected = item
User clicks 'Nach ALLEGRO übernehmen' → sendMessage(selected_result, 'textfield') → deep-clones result, attaches selected zahlungsempfaenger → JSON.stringify → WebSocket.send
WebSocket server receives → broadcasts to all clients
vue_textarea_realtime_sync: User types in textarea → v-model → internal_content_textarea reactive data updated
Vue watcher fires immediately on each change → sendMessage(val, 'textarea') → WebSocket.send({target:'textarea', content:val})
All WebSocket clients receive the message in real time

relationships.note: calls subscribe() in constructor, not stored as field
lambda body is the onEvent implementation
